en.extract:
The grey-sided bush warbler (Cettia brunnifrons) is a species of bush warbler (family Cettiidae). It was formerly included in the "Old World warbler" assemblage.

It is found from northern Pakistan to central China.
The phylogenetic relationships among the Cettia species are shown below, based on the data by the Cornell Lab of Ornithology.

It split from the Cetti's warbler about 5 million years ago.

The '''grey-sided bush warbler''' ('''''Cettia brunnifrons''''') is a [[species]] of bush warbler ([[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Cettiidae]]). It was formerly included in the "[[Old World warbler]]" assemblage.
[[File:CettiaBrunneifronsKeulemans.jpg|thumb|300px|right| Illustration by [[John Gerrard Keulemans|Keulemans]], 1881]]
It is found from northern [[Pakistan]] to central [[China]].

{{Phylogeny/Cettia}}It split from the [[Cetti's warbler]] about 5 million years ago.

zh.extract:
棕顶树莺（学名：Cettia brunnifrons）为鶲科树莺属的鸟类。该物种分布在南亚地区，其模式产地在尼泊尔。


亚种
棕顶树莺指名亚种（学名：Cettia brunnifrons brunnifrons）。在中国大陆，分布于西藏、甘肃、四川、云南等地。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。
棕顶树莺云南亚种（学名：Cettia brunnifrons umbraticus）。在中国大陆，分布于西藏、甘肃、四川、云南等地。该物种的模式产地在云南。
